title: daboecia cantabrica var. rosea
intro: Daboecia cantabrica var. rosea, commonly known as the Pyrenean heath or rose heath, is a flowering plant that belongs to the heath family, Ericaceae. The plant is native to the Pyrenees mountains and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The rose heath has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
